title: kalmia microphylla subsp. occidentalis
intro: Kalmia microphylla subsp. occidentalis, also known as western mountain laurel, is a shrub native to the western parts of North America, characterized by its small, pink to purple flowers and leathery leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of kalmia microphylla subsp. occidentalis include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and act as an antioxidant, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
